scope.setting: finite two-observer static-patch screen operator-transfer family with shared horizon algebra C^d
scope.result_type: single-dynamics screen-restricted no-go plus intrinsic completion theorem inside the finite family
scope.claim_boundary: finite QEC/OA-QEC dynamics benchmark; not continuum dS/CFT, de Sitter quantum gravity, type-III algebra, or ER=EPR in de Sitter

core_claim: Even when screen-visible data and the observer bridge channel are generated by one finite transfer process, screen-restricted dynamics can agree while algebraic bridge connectivity differs.

exact_theorem_style_claims.claim: For every d>=2, offdiag_coupling=1 and offdiag_coupling=0 in the same finite screen-transfer family agree on screen entropy, diagonal correlator, horizon-overlap, low-order screen correlator, and screen-restricted transfer data, but induce bridge algebras M_d and C^d.
Full operator transfer spectrum, off-diagonal relative-entropy response, and commutator/OTOC-style growth determine the bridge algebra inside this finite family.
The bridge channel and the screen shadow are both derived from the same finite operator-transfer process.

relationship_to_prior_goals.goal19: Complete Pauli response is sufficient in the one-qubit Pauli case.
relationship_to_prior_goals.goal20: Incomplete diagonal probes certify C^d while missing M_d.
relationship_to_prior_goals.goal21: Screen shadows can hide bridge algebra.
relationship_to_prior_goals.goal22: The separation persists when screen shadow and bridge channel are outputs of one finite transfer dynamics.

proof_obligations.claim: Emit the Goal 22 finite dS/CFT-ER=EPR single-dynamics certificate.
Validate the machine-readable Goal 22 certificate index.

expert_feedback_summary: A finite dS/CFT-inspired screen transfer process can generate identical screen-restricted dynamics while inducing different observer bridge algebras. The ER=EPR-like bridge datum appears in full intrinsic operator response and commutator growth, not in entropy or low-order screen correlators alone.
limitations: This is finite QEC/OA-QEC mathematics. It does not construct a continuum CFT dual, de Sitter quantum gravity, type-III observer algebra, or literal ER=EPR-in-dS theorem.
